intro: JavaScript's asynchronous callback and Promise model contrasts sharply with Python's synchronous-first style and `asyncio` library. Converting JavaScript to Python means replacing arrow functions with `def` or `lambda`, translating `Promise.all` to `asyncio.gather`, and swapping prototype-based class patterns for Python's class inheritance. Python's strict indentation replaces JavaScript's curly-brace blocks.

diffs.from: Promises + async/await over a non-blocking event loop.
diffs.to: asyncio with async/await; use threading or multiprocessing for CPU work.

diffs.from: ES Modules (import/export) or CommonJS (require). Config in package.json.
diffs.to: Unified import system. Third-party packages managed via pip + pyproject.toml.

diffs.from: null (intentional absence) and undefined (unset variable) coexist.
diffs.to: Only None. No undefined — accessing a missing key raises KeyError.

diffs.from: for...of, for...in (keys), forEach, and Array methods.
diffs.to: for, for...in (values), enumerate(), and list comprehensions.

diffs.from: throw/catch with Error objects. No checked exceptions.
diffs.to: raise/except with an exception hierarchy. Exception chaining via from.

examples.title: Async HTTP request
examples.description: JS fetch/Promise chains translate to Python httpx or aiohttp with async/await syntax.

examples.title: Filter and reshape an array of objects
examples.description: JS filter+map chains become Python list comprehensions with dict access.
examples.toCode:
users = [
    {"name": "Alice", "age": 30, "active": True},
    {"name": "Bob",   "age": 22, "active": False},
    {"name": "Carol", "age": 28, "active": True},
]

names = [
    u["name"] for u in users
    if u["active"] and u["age"] >= 25
]

print(names)  # ['Alice', 'Carol']

tips.title: Promise.all → asyncio.gather
tips.body: Replace Promise.all([p1, p2]) with await asyncio.gather(coro1(), coro2()). Both run coroutines concurrently and return results as a list.

tips.title: Arrow functions → lambda / def
tips.body: Single-expression arrows become lambdas: x => x * 2 → lambda x: x * 2. Multi-statement function bodies need a full def.

tips.title: Template literals → f-strings
tips.body: JS template literals use backticks with ${expr}. Python f-strings use f"..." with {expr}. The semantics are nearly identical.

tips.title: null / undefined → None
tips.body: Python has only None. JS code that checks for undefined needs to be rewritten as attribute existence checks (hasattr) or dict .get() calls.
